full_prompt: Design one representative 3D-printed single-use umbilical cord clamp compliant with the Field Ready humanitarian 3D-print library. The clamp is a hinged plastic jaw with a snap-lock closure approximately 50 mm long x 20 mm wide x 6 mm thick, printed in PLA (or medical-grade equivalent) on an FFF printer, used to occlude a newborn's umbilical cord after delivery. Functional requirement: when latched closed around a nominal 10 mm diameter umbilical cord, the clamp must sustain a minimum retention / closing force of 80 N across the jaw without disengaging the snap lock or fracturing any part, and must maintain that force for at least 24 hours of sustained loading (creep considered by using a reduced long-term PLA design strength). Safety factor of 2 per Field Ready design brief, so verification load is 160 N. Target material: PLA FFF-printed with bulk tensile yield ~50 MPa, design strength 25 MPa (accounting for anisotropy + creep reduction). Snap-lock geometry is a cantilever beam with a 1.5 mm x 2 mm latch barb engaging a mating pocket. Deliverable is a single CAD part (or 2-part snap assembly) suitable for linear static structural FEA at 160 N verification load with the jaw tips fixed and the hinge pin free. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required.
short_prompt:
Design a 3D-printed single-use hinged umbilical-cord clamp with a snap-lock closure per the Field Ready humanitarian 3D-print library. The clamp is printed in PLA (or medical-grade equivalent) on an FFF printer and latches around a nominal 10 mm umbilical cord. Sustained 24 h retention with creep-reduced PLA design strength 25 MPa (bulk yield ~50 MPa). Safety factor 2.0 per the Field Ready brief; snap must not disengage below the verification load.

Envelope: ~50 x 20 x 6 mm hinged jaw with a cantilever snap barb (1.5 x 2.0 mm latch into a mating pocket).

Load cases.
- LC1 retention nominal: 80 N closing force across the jaw.
- LC2 retention factored (SF 2): 160 N verification load, jaw tips fixed and hinge pinned.

prompt.title: Field Ready 3D-printed umbilical cord clamp
prompt.brief: Design a PLA 3D-printed umbilical cord clamp retaining 80 N closing force with SF 2 (verified to 160 N).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: PLA design strength including creep + anisotropy
snap must not release below 2x design load

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Not applicable (though sterilization is a separate concern).

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Biocompatibility of FFF-PLA for clinical use
